title: http: fix use-after-free when freeParser is called during llhttp_execute
category: re-entrancy use-after-free (parser freed while llhttp_execute is on the stack)
crash_site: SIGSEGV / use-after-free in the llhttp parser callbacks (parserOnHeadersComplete / parserOnMessageComplete in src/node_http_parser.cc) when they resume after the parser's state was nullified mid-parse. Those callbacks are correct; the parser was freed underneath them.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the llhttp callbacks where it crashes (parserOnHeadersComplete etc.) — correct; cleanParser (it just nullifies, as designed). The bug is freeParser running cleanParser() while llhttp_execute() is still on the stack (re-entrancy via a synchronous 'close' handler during pipelined parsing).
ground_truth.fix_summary: When pipelined requests arrive in one TCP segment, llhttp_execute() parses them sequentially; a synchronous 'close' handler fired mid-parse can call freeParser(), which immediately ran cleanParser() and nullified parser state while llhttp_execute() was still on the call stack -> subsequent callbacks dereferenced freed parser members (UAF). Fix: in freeParser() call parser.remove() before cleanParser() (let the parser stack unwind first), and add an is_being_freed_ flag (set by markFreed()) that the C++ callback dispatcher Proxy::Raw checks to abort llhttp callbacks early.

admission.note: monogram+haiku vs baseline+haiku discrimination. HARD: ASAN/crash is in llhttp callbacks, but the cause is freeParser() nullifying parser state mid-llhttp_execute via a re-entrant synchronous close handler during pipelined parsing. Live decoy = the crashing callbacks. Authored from PR #62095 fix diff (reorder remove() before cleanParser; is_being_freed_ guard in Proxy::Raw).

provenance: https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/62095 — http: fix UAF when freeParser is called during llhttp_execute (merged 2026-03-06); fix commit a06e789625ec; base 8edeff9aa715. Root cause + fix from the commit diff (freeParser reorder + is_being_freed_ in node_http_parser.cc).
